summary: Verified fix for user permission toggle persistence bug (stale closure in React state). All backend API tests (8/8) and UI tests passed. Permission toggles (hide_cash_expected, hide_recent_orders) now correctly persist after save using functional state updates.

issues: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (accessibility warning in console)

critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED FIX: Settings.js line 1445-1454 toggleUserPermission uses functional state update setEditUserForm(prev => ...) preventing stale closure
VERIFIED FIX: Settings.js line 2839-2846 inline onCheckedChange also uses functional state update pattern
Backend PUT /api/users/{id} correctly accepts and persists permissions array
data-testid attributes properly added: edit-user-form, edit-user-save-btn, edit-user-cancel-btn, edit-user-btn-{id}
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test cashier user
context_for_next_testing_agent: Permission toggle persistence bug is FIXED. The fix uses functional state updates (setEditUserForm(prev => ...)) instead of direct state reference to prevent stale closure. Test cashier user ID: 29d01373-293c-4703-8c4f-2f832d9d2abb. Current permissions restored to: pos, tables, orders, hide_cash_expected, hide_recent_orders, expenses, delivery.

verified_bug_fix.bug_description: Permission toggles (hide_cash_expected, hide_recent_orders) in edit user dialog didn't persist correctly due to stale closure bug in React state management
verified_bug_fix.root_cause: Using direct state reference setEditUserForm({...editUserForm, permissions: newPerms}) captured stale state in closure
verified_bug_fix.fix_applied: Changed to functional state update setEditUserForm(prev => ({...prev, permissions: newPerms})) which always uses latest state
verification_steps: 3. Click edit on test cashier user
6. Re-open edit dialog - both toggles are OFF (persisted)
9. Re-open edit dialog - both toggles are ON (persisted)

console_warnings: IndexedDB is_synced index error (unrelated to this bug)
